response.content: Use a Python CodeAct script, not plain prose. Under the directory "/tmp/ironclaw_live_codeact_shims/js_codemod_use_strict_shim", find every `.js` file recursively under `src/`. For each file, prepend the literal line `'use strict';\n` to its content. Preserve every other byte of the existing content exactly (do not add extra whitespace, blank lines, or remove the trailing newline). After updating every file, call FINAL exactly with `done=ok`. IMPORTANT: this is the SHIM variant, so prefer `await find_files('**/*.js', path=...)`, `await read_text(path)`, and `await write_text(path, content)`. The text shims return and accept raw file content directly with no line-number framing. All shims are async, so always use `await`.

for path in files:

    new_content = "'use strict';\n" + content
    await write_text(path, new_content)

FINAL("done=ok")

response.content:
```repl
base = "/tmp/ironclaw_live_codeact_shims/js_codemod_use_strict_shim"
files = await find_files("src/**/*.js", path=base)

for rel_path in files:
    path = base + "/" + rel_path
    content = await read_text(path)
    if isinstance(content, dict):
        content = content.get("text", content.get("content", ""))
    new_content = "'use strict';\n" + content
    await write_text(path, new_content)

FINAL("done=ok")
```
